overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Out-of-state prescribers may prescribe controlled substances, provided they meet strict CA paper form requirements or utilize e-prescribing.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: CII prescriptions must be transmitted electronically per AB 2789.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: AB 2789 requires nearly all prescriptions in CA to be e-prescribed.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Telehealth prescribing of controlled substances generally requires a prior in-person visit per federal standards, with limited emergency state exceptions.
overrides.special_restrictions: CA specific controlled substance prescription security forms are mandatory if an exception to e-prescribing applies.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Widespread but restricted for initial controlled substance prescriptions.
access_facts.notes: Extremely high volume state for traveling nurses requires proactive e-prescribing setup before arrival.

rules.rule_text: All prescriptions must be electronically transmitted directly to the dispensing pharmacy.
Pharmacists must consult the CURES database prior to dispensing a Schedule II-IV controlled substance.

restrictions.description: Paper prescriptions are rejected unless written on specialized CA security forms.
restrictions.workaround: Have the prescriber send the prescription electronically.

segment_data.key_friction: Strict e-prescribing mandate and unique paper security forms make relying on out-of-state paper scripts impossible.

items.guidance_text: Ensure your out-of-state prescriber is equipped to send E-scripts across state lines before you travel.

segment_data.key_friction: Pharmacists cannot accept standard out-of-state paper prescriptions.

items.guidance_text: Do not bring physical prescriptions to California. Tell your provider to use electronic prescribing.

claims.claim_text: California mandates electronic prescribing for all prescriptions.
CURES check is mandatory prior to dispensing.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: New York pharmacists can legally fill out-of-state prescriptions, but I-STOP strict e-prescribing mandates apply universally.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: CII prescriptions must be transmitted electronically per state law.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Every prescription issued in New York must be transmitted electronically.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Prescribers utilizing telehealth must abide by state and federal laws, generally limiting controlled substance issuance without physical evaluations.
overrides.emergency_supply_text: A 5-day emergency supply is permitted for certain controlled substances under strict guidelines.
overrides.board_of_pharmacy: New York State Board of Pharmacy
overrides.special_restrictions: I-STOP registry check is required prior to prescribing and dispensing most controlled substances.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial prescribing of controlled substances.
access_facts.notes: Extremely strict registry requirements frequently block casual travelers.

rules.rule_text: I-STOP requires practitioners to electronically prescribe all medications.

restrictions.description: Paper prescriptions are generally invalid under I-STOP laws.
restrictions.workaround: Practitioners must use compliant E-prescribing platforms.

segment_data.key_friction: Out of state providers must successfully connect to NY dispensing systems.

items.guidance_text: Ensure your home state prescriber supports out-of-state e-prescribing networks.

claims.claim_text: I-STOP enforces a universal electronic prescribing mandate.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Pharmacists may fill controlled substances from contiguous and non-contiguous states subject to verification.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: CII prescriptions must be transmitted electronically per state law.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Electronic prescribing is required for all controlled substances in Illinois.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Telehealth rules generally align with federal DEA laws regarding initial in-person evaluations.
overrides.board_of_pharmacy: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
overrides.special_restrictions: Documentation of out-of-state prescriber credentials may be heavily scrutinized.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial controlled substance prescribing via telehealth.
access_facts.notes: E-prescribing strictness for controlled substances complicates cross-border dispensing.

rules.rule_text: Schedule II prescriptions must be e-prescribed and cannot be refilled.

restrictions.description: Illinois generally mandates electronic prescribing for all controlled substances.

items.guidance_text: If prescribed out-of-state, ensure the provider has transmitted the e-rx to your specific Illinois pharmacy accurately.

claims.claim_text: Illinois PMP checks are required prior to dispensing certain controlled substances.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Prescriptions from out-of-state prescribers are valid if they conform to Pennsylvania's stringent e-prescribing rules.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Act 96 mandates electronic prescribing for all Schedule II-V controlled substances.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Prescribing controlled substances typically requires a face-to-face encounter under federal/state regulations.
overrides.special_restrictions: Act 96 establishes firm e-prescribing requirements for controlled substances.

access_facts.notes: E-rx mandate creates the most friction for out-of-state prescriptions.

rules.rule_text: Act 96 mandates the electronic transmission of Schedule II through V controlled substances.

restrictions.description: Paper prescriptions are not accepted for controlled substances without a specific statutory override.

segment_data.key_friction: Strict e-prescribing and monitoring programs restrict casual out of state prescription deposits.

items.guidance_text: Be prepared to encounter delays if your prescriber utilizes paper scripts, which are heavily restricted in PA.

claims.claim_text: Pennsylvania enforces electronic prescribing via Act 96.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Pharmacists can dispense out-of-state valid prescriptions, but strict checks in OARRS are mandatory.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Ohio mandates electronic prescribing for all controlled substances.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Controlled substances prescribed via telehealth are restricted, closely tracking DEA guidelines for in-person evaluations.
overrides.board_of_pharmacy: State of Ohio Board of Pharmacy
overrides.special_restrictions: Mandatory query of OARRS system required before dispensing opiate and benzodiazepine medications.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial controlled substance prescribing via telehealth.
access_facts.notes: Extensive tracking restricts fast cross-state dispensation.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacists are legally obligated to query OARRS prior to dispensing an outpatient controlled substance.

restrictions.description: Electronic prescribing is required for schedule II controlled substances.

segment_data.key_friction: OARRS tracking cross-state can cause prescription verification delays.

items.guidance_text: OARRS closely tracks controlled substances. Out of state prescribers should utilize e-prescribing platforms that integrate smoothly.

claims.claim_text: OARRS system mandatory review required prior to dispensing controlled formulations.
